premises.content: For low-temperature correlated metals, the empirical Kadowaki-Woods diagnostic compares the resistivity coefficient A from rho(T)=rho_0 + A T^2 with the Sommerfeld coefficient gamma from C(T)=gamma T+..., and A/gamma^2 near 1e-5 muOmega cm (mol K/mJ)^2 is treated by the selected LKM chain as evidence for standard correlated Fermi-liquid phenomenology in heavy-fermion and valence-fluctuating materials [@Paramanik2013].
premises.label: gcn_2dc55af_kw_empirical_scale

premises.content: For a metal with temperature-independent low-temperature molar susceptibility chi_FL and Sommerfeld coefficient gamma, the selected LKM chain treats the Wilson-Sommerfeld ratio R_W=(pi^2 k_B^2 chi_FL)/(gamma mu_eff^2) as a correlated-Fermi-liquid consistency check when mu_eff is normalized either to the Ce3+ free-ion moment 2.54 mu_B or to the conduction-electron reference value 1.73 mu_B [@Paramanik2013].
premises.label: gcn_7459a446_wilson_ratio_interpretation
